qn1: Is it rude to sneak out and ask a nurse if they have any advice?
qn2: How to deal with loud roommate?
narrative: This might sound dumb but I've been in hospital 4 nights already and am meant to be going home tomorrow (I hope!). My roommate from the statt is lovely and I'm not sure if it was because I was so out of it or because she didn't snore but I haven't heard her snore before. Tonight she snores like a chainsaw and I CANNOT sleep at all. She's also developed a hacking cough that I think is related. It's my last night but if I don't sleep and am not well enough they might decide to keep me longer which I don't want. I also really need my sleep because I need to her better. The nurses are kinda bitchty and rude and I feel like they might say something loudly to or in front of my roommate if I complain. I can put up with a sleepless night normally but not when I'm this sick.
events: My roommate snores like a chainsaw tonight.
My roommate developed a hacking cough.

qn1: Should I stay in computer science or do Management information System?
qn2: What to do after graduating college with a Bachelor of Technology in Computer Systems?
narrative: Hello /r/Advice  I'm currently pursuing a Bachelor of Technology in Computer Systems (Software Development Track). This summer will be the start of my last year in college. So I was thinking what I should do afterwards. Clearly looking for a job is top priority but I wish to continue my studies. Currently my college does not provide a Computer Science B.S degree. So transferring to another college would seem logical. I love computer science and I would most certainly like having a degree on it. or do I have to enroll in a regular undergraduate level to complete a Bachelor of Science in Computer Science from the start and then move on to a Masters in Computer Science. I don't know if I'm making much sense. Thought maybe the Internet can help me. =/
events: I am currently pursuing a Bachelor of Technology in Computer Systems (Software Development Track).
This summer will be the start of my last year in college.
I was thinking what I should do afterwards.
I thought maybe the Internet can help me.
